diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dysuria is a cardinal symptom of lower-tract infection.
Urethritis also causes dysuria; distinguished by discharge, sexual history and dipstick.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urinary frequency and urgency, small voided volumes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bladder mucosal inflammation drives urgency and frequency with small volumes.
Haemorrhagic cystitis produces visible haematuria; supports infection when accompanied by infective symptoms and dipstick findings.
Visible haematuria is the cardinal red flag for urothelial malignancy if it persists after the infection is treated.
Stones also cause haematuria, but usually with colicky flank pain rather than infective dipstick findings.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic ache with sensation of incomplete emptying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Suprapubic discomfort and incomplete-emptying sensation localise the process to the bladder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent sexual intercourse without post-coital voiding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A classic mechanical precipitant of post-coital cystitis in women.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, rigors and flank pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile, no rigors and no flank pain make ascending upper-tract infection unlikely — confines the process to the lower tract.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care urine dipstick positive for leukocyte esterase and nitrites, with blood

investigation_details.expected_result: Positive leukocyte esterase and nitrites; blood present
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without urinalysis the infective basis is presumptive only; cannot confirm bacteriuria or guide treatment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nitrites are relatively specific for Enterobacteriaceae; with leukocyte esterase they strongly support bacterial UTI. Clinician point-of-care test — not obtainable via telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of costovertebral-angle tenderness on percussion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative CVA tenderness argues against renal involvement — a clinician examination finding, not obtainable remotely.

investigation_details.investigation_name: Midstream urine microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_details.expected_result: Significant bacteriuria, likely coliform (e.g. E. coli), with antibiotic sensitivities
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed resistance and missed confirmation of the organism; culture is recommended where there is haematuria or treatment failure.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Culture confirms the organism and directs therapy — laboratory investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute lower urinary tract infection syndrome
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Dysuria + frequency/urgency + suprapubic discomfort + positive dipstick is the classic cystitis cluster.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line bedside confirmation of infection; requires in-person same-day assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Midstream urine microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Indicated given macroscopic haematuria; confirms organism and sensitivities.
Reproductive-age patient with unknown pregnancy status — result changes antibiotic selection and follow-up intensity.
Not indicated acutely; reserved for visible haematuria that fails to resolve after the infection is treated, via urology referral. Over-ordering acutely is unnecessary.
